title: lavandula multifida var. pinnata
intro: Lavandula multifida var. pinnata, also known as pinched lavender, is a subspecies of the Lavandula multifida plant, which is a flowering plant in the mint family native to the southwestern regions of Western Australia and southwestern Western Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as reduce pain and promote relaxation. The health benefits of pinched lavender include its ability to aid in stress relief, improve sleep quality, and support the immune system, making it a popular herb for aromatherapy and herbal remedies. Pinched lavender can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and salves, which can be applied topically or inhaled to reap its benefits.
